notes: Visual phase partially run: dev server unreachable (net::ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE) for every localhost:8765 GET during the audit. CDN spot-check for nextwave/screenshot-024-v2-b.jpg returned 200. Lightbox markup + script present. Section-label contiguity verified by file read.

findings.issue:
Line 188:
03 · Reach & Adoption
— double-encoded HTML entity (& escaped twice) will render visibly as 'Reach & Adoption' on the page, not 'Reach & Adoption'. Same bug also present in cortex/index.html:200 (Alerts & Escalations) — strongly suggests the renderer is escaping '&' a second time after the YAML already passed it as '&'.
findings.fix: Strip the redundant 'amp;' in the rendered HTML and audit build_impetus.py's escape logic. The YAML at data/impetus/nextwave.yaml line 34 has 'scale_label: Reach & Adoption' — renderer is double-encoding it on output.

findings.issue: Paragraph 2 (lines 178-183) repeats 'leadership direction' twice in three sentences: 'Low active usage reflects the leadership direction: moving from 400 human designers manually browsing trends to AI-native flows...' followed by 'Leadership direction: move toward AI-generated designs where human designers curate rather than create from scratch.' The second sentence near-duplicates the first.
findings.fix: Drop the repeating second sentence (line 182-183). The first sentence already states the direction; the duplication reads as filler. Trim the section to a single 3-sentence paragraph.

findings.issue: Hero pill = 'Live' but the page itself reports active usage at ~19% (~81 of ~424). The page handles this honestly in §03 caption ('Active usage low and falling on purpose...'), but a Live pill on a tool with 19% active engagement is a borderline overclaim. The mitigation in prose is good; consider tagging the pill itself.
findings.fix: Either keep 'Live' (defensible — the platform is in production) or qualify with 'Live · low daily-use by design'. The prose explanation must stay regardless.

findings.issue: Hero H1 (line 163) reads 'NextWave (Fashion Trends Forecasting).' and the sub-line directly below (line 164) reads '(Trends Analysis · Fashion Trends Forecasting)'. 'Fashion Trends Forecasting' appears twice in two adjacent lines.
findings.fix: Drop 'Fashion Trends Forecasting' from the sub-line; leave just '(Trends Analysis)' or drop the sub-line entirely since the H1 already says the long form.

findings.issue: Line 244 caption alt-text starts with lowercase 'attributes' — 'attributes· Body type, brand, color and fit distribution charts.' Alongside the missing-space-before-'·' renderer artefact present elsewhere.
findings.fix: Capitalise to 'Attributes' and apply the same caption-split-renderer fix used for cortex (CTX-4).

findings.issue: Caption alt-texts on lines 223, 230, 237, 244 all carry the 'Title· Description' artefact (no space before the '·'). Identical pattern to cortex CTX-4 and master-hub MH-7 — same root cause.
findings.fix: One-pass renderer fix in build_impetus.py: insert a space around the '·' separator when splitting composite captions.

findings.issue: impetus/index.html line 248 'Plan' band names this surface 'NextWave (trends)' — lower-case 'trends' parenthetical. The page itself uses '(Trends Analysis · Fashion Trends Forecasting)' as the H1 sub-line. Convention drift but not a name change.
findings.fix: Pick one and apply consistently — either '(trends)' across all references or the longer parenthetical. Recommend keeping the value-chain band tight ('NextWave') and dropping the parenthetical there.

strengths: Section labels are contiguous (hero · 02 · 03 · 04 · 05); no gap from the deleted §05 Updates Shipped + Sources sections.

Honesty about ~19% active usage is preserved in the §02 prose and §03 caption — the page does not hide the low engagement, it explains why ('AI-native flows where fewer designers curate AI-generated outputs').
Screenshot URLs use the -v2/-v2-b versioned pattern; CDN spot-check returns 200; lightbox markup + script present at the bottom of the page.
§03 chip strip names the actual sub-products (Fashion Eye · Celebrity Corner · Regional Trends · Ajio trending queries) — gives an Apex reader a navigable mental model of what's inside the platform.
Page title and breadcrumb both carry the renamed 'NextWave (Fashion Trends Forecasting)' form, matching the impetus/index.html card title.
